[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]

Neighbouring languages integrate elements of [[Aelaki]] morphology to varying degrees.

== Borrowed Ki in Neknek ==
While Neknek never re-grammaticalised [[Ki_syllables|Ki]] as agreement markers, speakers append them '''phonotactically''' to certain loans — especially personal names and plant terms.

=== Sapling loan example ===
{| class="wikitable"
! Language !! Singular !! Partitive plural !! Collective
|-
| '''Aelaki''' || '''Basolush''' || '''Basosolush''' || '''Baselish'''
|-
| '''Neknek''' || '''Basolush''' || '''Basolushk''' (Ki-paucal) || '''Baselish'''
|}

Neknek only applies '''reduplicative plurals''' inside explicitly Aelaki-derived stems.

== Other contact zones ==
* The umlaut [[Collective|collective]] has diffused into several unrelated language families as a prestige marker for '''totality'''.
* [[Numerals]] 1-10 (with partitive/collective alternants) are pan-regional trade jargon.

== See also ==
* [[Ki_syllables]]
* [[Numerals]]
* [[Collective]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
